description: Devastating rebellion that killed millions and permanently weakened Tang Dynasty China.

name: great-heathen-army
description: A massive Viking force invaded England, conquering most Anglo-Saxon kingdoms.

name: battle-of-hastings
description: William the Conqueror defeated King Harold II, beginning Norman rule of England.

name: battle-of-stamford-bridge
description: King Harold defeated a Norse invasion just days before facing the Normans at Hastings.

name: capture-of-jerusalem-1099
description: Crusaders stormed Jerusalem, establishing a Christian kingdom in the Holy Land.

name: battle-of-legnano
description: The Lombard League defeated Emperor Frederick Barbarossa, securing Italian city independence.

name: battle-of-hattin
description: Saladin crushed the Crusader army, leaving Jerusalem defenseless.

name: saladin-recaptures-jerusalem
description: Saladin retook Jerusalem from the Crusaders after 88 years of Christian rule.

name: battle-of-arsuf
description: Richard the Lionheart defeated Saladin's forces during the Third Crusade.

name: albigensian-crusade-begins
description: Pope Innocent III launched a crusade against Cathar heretics in southern France.

name: mongol-invasion-jin
description: Genghis Khan invaded northern China, beginning the Mongol conquest of the Jin Dynasty.

name: childrens-crusade
description: Thousands of young people marched to reclaim the Holy Land, most dying or being enslaved.

name: battle-of-bouvines
description: Philip II of France defeated England and the Holy Roman Empire, establishing French dominance.

name: battle-of-legnica
description: Mongols annihilated a European army, spreading terror across Christendom.

name: battle-of-ain-jalut
description: Mamluks defeated the Mongols, halting their advance into the Middle East and Africa.

name: kamikaze-mongol-fleet
description: A typhoon destroyed the Mongol invasion fleet, saving Japan and inspiring the 'divine wind' legend.

name: battle-of-golden-spurs
description: Flemish militia defeated French knights at Courtrai, collecting golden spurs from the fallen.

name: battle-of-bannockburn
description: Robert the Bruce's Scottish forces defeated the English army, securing Scottish independence.

name: battle-of-sluys
description: English longbowmen destroyed the French fleet, gaining naval supremacy in the Hundred Years' War.

name: peasants-revolt-england
description: English peasants led by Wat Tyler marched on London, demanding an end to serfdom.

name: battle-of-kosovo
description: Ottomans defeated the Serbs in a battle that became central to Serbian national identity.

name: battle-of-grunwald
description: Poland-Lithuania crushed the Teutonic Knights, ending their Baltic expansion.

name: joan-of-arc-executed
description: The French heroine was burned at the stake by the English after a trial for heresy.

name: battle-of-nancy
description: Swiss forces killed Charles the Bold, ending Burgundy as an independent power.

name: battle-of-bosworth-field
description: Henry Tudor defeated Richard III, ending the Wars of the Roses and founding the Tudor dynasty.

name: battle-of-flodden
description: England defeated Scotland, killing King James IV and much of the Scottish nobility.

name: battle-of-marignano
description: Francis I of France defeated the Swiss, conquering Milan and establishing French dominance in Italy.

name: fall-of-tenochtitlan
description: Hernán Cortés and his indigenous allies conquered the Aztec capital, ending the Aztec Empire.

name: hyksos-invasion-of-egypt
description: Semitic peoples from Western Asia conquered Lower Egypt, establishing the 15th Dynasty and introducing horse-drawn chariots to Egyptian warfare.

name: pompey-conquers-jerusalem
description: Roman general Pompey captured Jerusalem during the Third Mithridatic War, ending the Hasmonean Kingdom's independence and bringing Judea under Roman control.

name: roman-conquest-of-britain
description: Emperor Claudius launched the successful Roman invasion of Britain, establishing the province of Britannia that would last nearly four centuries.

name: clovis-defeats-visigoths-at-vouille
description: Frankish King Clovis I defeated the Visigoths, killing King Alaric II and driving the Visigoths from most of Gaul into Hispania.

name: battle-of-badr
description: The first major military victory for Muslims, where Prophet Muhammad's forces defeated a larger Meccan army, marking a turning point in early Islamic history.

name: conquest-of-mecca
description: Prophet Muhammad led 10,000 followers to capture Mecca largely without bloodshed, converting the Kaaba into Islam's holiest site.

name: battle-of-yarmouk
description: Arab Muslim forces decisively defeated the Byzantine Empire, leading to the loss of the Levant and opening the path for Islamic expansion.

name: arab-conquest-of-egypt
description: Amr ibn al-As led Arab forces to conquer Byzantine Egypt, ending over 900 years of Greco-Roman rule and beginning the Islamization of the region.

name: umayyad-conquest-of-spain
description: Tariq ibn Ziyad led Umayyad forces across Gibraltar and defeated the Visigoths, beginning nearly 800 years of Islamic presence in Iberia.

name: battle-of-talas
description: Abbasid and Tibetan forces defeated Tang China in Central Asia. Captured Chinese papermakers helped spread papermaking technology westward.

name: alfred-the-great-recaptures-london
description: King Alfred of Wessex recaptured London from the Vikings, restoring the city and strengthening Anglo-Saxon resistance to Danish invasion.

name: fourth-crusade-called
description: Pope Innocent III called for a new crusade to recapture Jerusalem, which infamously diverted to sack Constantinople instead.

name: sixth-crusade-treaty
description: Emperor Frederick II negotiated the Treaty of Jaffa with Sultan al-Kamil, peacefully recovering Jerusalem for Christians without major combat.

name: siege-of-montsegur
description: French crusaders besieged the Cathar stronghold for ten months. Over 200 Cathars were burned at the stake after surrendering.

name: jacquerie-peasant-revolt
description: French peasants rose up against the nobility during the Hundred Years' War, attacking castles and manors before being brutally suppressed.

name: battle-of-varna
description: Ottoman forces defeated a crusading army led by King Władysław III of Poland, who was killed in battle, ending the Crusade of Varna.

name: edward-iv-crowned
description: Edward of York became King of England after defeating Lancastrian forces at Towton, the bloodiest battle in English history during the Wars of the Roses.

name: german-peasants-war-begins
description: Peasants across German-speaking lands rose up demanding economic and religious reforms. The revolt was crushed with over 100,000 peasants killed.

name: st-bartholomews-day-massacre
description: Catholic mobs massacred thousands of Huguenots across France in coordinated attacks beginning in Paris, escalating the French Wars of Religion.

name: grotius-publishes-on-the-law-of-war-and-peace
friendly_name: Grotius' Law of War and Peace
description: Dutch jurist Hugo Grotius published his foundational work on international law, establishing principles for just war and the laws governing armed conflict.

name: irish-rebellion-of-1641
description: Irish Catholics rose against English and Scottish Protestant settlers, leading to widespread violence and eventually Cromwell's brutal conquest of Ireland.

name: franco-dutch-war-begins
description: Louis XIV invaded the Dutch Republic in the Year of Disaster. The Dutch flooded their lands and formed alliances to resist French expansion.

name: king-philips-war
description: Native American leader Metacom (King Philip) led an alliance against New England colonists in one of the bloodiest conflicts per capita in American history.

name: siege-of-derry
description: Protestant defenders held the city of Derry for 105 days against Jacobite forces loyal to the deposed Catholic King James II.

name: battle-of-the-boyne
description: William III of Orange defeated the deposed James II in Ireland, securing Protestant rule in Britain and becoming a symbol of Ulster Protestant identity.

name: war-of-spanish-succession-begins
description: European powers went to war over the Spanish throne after Charles II died without an heir, reshaping the balance of power for decades.

name: nadir-shah-sacks-delhi
description: Persian ruler Nadir Shah invaded Mughal India and plundered Delhi, taking the Peacock Throne and Koh-i-Noor diamond among vast treasures.

name: war-of-jenkins-ear
description: Britain declared war on Spain after Captain Jenkins displayed his severed ear in Parliament, claiming Spanish coastguards had cut it off.

name: war-of-austrian-succession-begins
description: European powers challenged Maria Theresa's right to inherit Habsburg lands, sparking a continent-wide war that lasted eight years.

name: battles-of-lexington-and-concord
description: The first military engagements of the American Revolution, where colonial militiamen fired the 'shot heard round the world' against British troops.

name: shays-rebellion
description: Massachusetts farmers led by Daniel Shays rebelled against debt collection and taxation, highlighting weaknesses in the Articles of Confederation.

name: irish-rebellion-of-1798
description: Irish republicans inspired by the French Revolution rose against British rule. The failed uprising led to the Act of Union with Britain.

name: war-of-1812-begins
description: The United States declared war on Britain over maritime disputes and expansionist aims, leading to the burning of Washington and the writing of the Star-Spangled Banner.

name: peterloo-massacre
description: British cavalry charged a crowd of 60,000 peaceful reformers in Manchester demanding parliamentary representation, killing 15 and injuring hundreds.

name: battle-of-ayacucho
description: South American independence forces decisively defeated Spanish royalists in Peru, effectively ending Spanish colonial rule on the continent.

name: battle-of-navarino
description: British, French, and Russian fleets destroyed the Ottoman-Egyptian fleet in Greece, the last major naval battle fought entirely with sailing ships.

name: nat-turners-rebellion
description: Enslaved preacher Nat Turner led a violent uprising in Virginia, killing about 60 white people before being captured and executed.

name: sepoy-mutiny
description: Indian soldiers rebelled against the British East India Company, triggering a widespread uprising that led to direct British Crown rule over India.

name: sand-creek-massacre
description: Colorado militia attacked a peaceful Cheyenne and Arapaho encampment, killing over 150 Native Americans, mostly women, children, and elderly.

name: nez-perce-war
description: Chief Joseph led the Nez Perce on a 1,170-mile fighting retreat toward Canada before surrendering with his famous speech 'I will fight no more forever.'

name: war-of-the-pacific
description: Chile fought Bolivia and Peru over nitrate-rich territories, winning coastal lands that left Bolivia landlocked to this day.

name: first-boer-war
description: Boer settlers defeated British forces in South Africa, winning independence for the Transvaal Republic after the Battle of Majuba Hill.

name: assassination-of-alexander-ii
description: Russian Tsar Alexander II, the 'Liberator' who freed the serfs, was killed by a bomb thrown by revolutionary terrorists in St. Petersburg.

name: assassination-of-garfield
description: President James Garfield was shot by a disappointed office-seeker, leading to civil service reform and the Pendleton Act.

name: jack-the-ripper-murders
description: An unidentified serial killer murdered at least five women in London's Whitechapel district, becoming history's most infamous unsolved case.

name: wounded-knee-massacre
description: US Army troops killed approximately 300 Lakota Sioux at Wounded Knee, South Dakota, marking the end of the American Indian Wars.

name: first-sino-japanese-war
description: Japan defeated Qing China over influence in Korea, gaining Taiwan and establishing itself as a major East Asian power.

name: philippine-american-war
description: Filipino independence fighters battled American forces after the US acquired the Philippines from Spain, resulting in hundreds of thousands of Filipino deaths.

name: assassination-of-mckinley
description: President William McKinley was shot by anarchist Leon Czolgosz at the Pan-American Exposition, making Theodore Roosevelt president.

name: end-of-philippine-american-war
description: The US declared victory in the Philippine-American War, though guerrilla resistance continued for years in parts of the archipelago.

name: kishinev-pogrom
description: A violent anti-Jewish riot in Kishinev, Russia killed 49 Jews and destroyed hundreds of homes, sparking international outrage and increased Jewish emigration.

name: herero-genocide
description: German colonial forces systematically killed tens of thousands of Herero and Nama people in German South-West Africa, considered the first genocide of the modern era.

name: potemkin-mutiny
description: Sailors on the Russian battleship Potemkin mutinied during the Russian Revolution, later immortalized in Eisenstein's influential silent film.

name: first-balkan-war
description: The Balkan League of Serbia, Greece, Bulgaria, and Montenegro attacked the Ottoman Empire, driving it out of most of its European territories.

name: second-balkan-war
description: Former allies turned on Bulgaria over disputed Macedonian territory, resulting in Bulgaria's defeat and increased tensions leading to World War I.

name: archduke-franz-ferdinand-assassinated
description: Serbian nationalist Gavrilo Princip shot the Austro-Hungarian heir in Sarajevo, triggering the chain of events that started World War I.

name: first-battle-of-the-marne
description: French and British forces halted the German advance on Paris, ending hopes for a quick victory and beginning four years of trench warfare.

name: armenian-genocide-begins
description: Ottoman authorities began the systematic deportation and massacre of Armenians, killing an estimated 1.5 million people.

name: second-battle-of-ypres-first-gas-attack
description: Germany deployed chlorine gas against Allied troops in Belgium, the first large-scale use of chemical weapons in modern warfare.

name: us-enters-world-war-i
description: The United States declared war on Germany after unrestricted submarine warfare and the Zimmermann Telegram, tipping the balance toward Allied victory.

name: third-afghan-war
description: Afghanistan invaded British India, leading to a brief war that resulted in full Afghan independence from British influence.

name: polish-soviet-war
description: Poland defeated the Soviet Red Army at the Battle of Warsaw, halting communist expansion into Europe and securing Polish independence.

name: turkish-war-of-independence
description: Mustafa Kemal Ataturk led Turkish nationalists to defeat Greek and Allied forces, establishing the modern Republic of Turkey.

name: invasion-of-poland
description: Nazi Germany invaded Poland on September 1st, prompting Britain and France to declare war and beginning World War II in Europe.

name: katyn-massacre
description: Soviet secret police executed approximately 22,000 Polish military officers, police, and intellectuals in the Katyn Forest and other sites.

name: trotsky-assassinated
description: Soviet agent Ramon Mercader killed exiled revolutionary Leon Trotsky in Mexico with an ice axe on Stalin's orders.

name: battle-of-the-philippine-sea
description: US Navy destroyed Japanese naval aviation in the 'Great Marianas Turkey Shoot,' securing air superiority in the Pacific War.

name: dresden-bombing
description: Allied firebombing of Dresden killed approximately 25,000 people and destroyed the historic city center, becoming one of WWII's most controversial attacks.

name: french-indochina-war-begins
description: Ho Chi Minh's Viet Minh began armed resistance against French colonial rule, starting an eight-year war that ended with French defeat at Dien Bien Phu.

name: first-arab-israeli-war
description: Arab states invaded the newly declared State of Israel, which successfully defended itself and expanded its territory beyond the UN partition plan.

name: tibet-invasion
description: The People's Republic of China invaded Tibet, defeating the small Tibetan army and incorporating Tibet into the PRC.

name: cia-coup-in-iran
description: The CIA and MI6 orchestrated a coup that overthrew democratically elected Prime Minister Mosaddegh, restoring Shah Pahlavi to power.

name: east-german-uprising
description: Workers in East Berlin and across East Germany rose up against the communist government before Soviet tanks crushed the rebellion.

name: algerian-war-begins
description: The FLN launched attacks against French rule in Algeria, beginning a brutal eight-year war that ended with Algerian independence.

name: cia-coup-in-guatemala
description: The CIA overthrew democratically elected President Arbenz to protect United Fruit Company interests, beginning decades of military rule and civil war.

name: great-leap-forward
description: Mao launched a radical campaign to rapidly industrialize China, resulting in history's deadliest famine with an estimated 30-45 million deaths.

name: sharpeville-massacre
description: South African police killed 69 Black protesters demonstrating against pass laws, galvanizing international opposition to apartheid.

name: south-vietnam-coup
description: South Vietnamese generals overthrew and assassinated President Ngo Dinh Diem with tacit US approval, destabilizing the country during the Vietnam War.

name: malcolm-x-assassinated
description: Black nationalist leader Malcolm X was shot dead in New York by members of the Nation of Islam, from which he had recently split.

name: che-guevara-killed
description: Revolutionary Che Guevara was captured and executed by Bolivian forces with CIA assistance while trying to foment revolution in South America.

name: tlatelolco-massacre
description: Mexican security forces killed hundreds of student protesters in Mexico City's Tlatelolco plaza, days before the Mexico City Olympics.

name: indo-pakistani-war-1971
description: India intervened in East Pakistan's independence struggle, defeating Pakistan in two weeks and enabling the creation of Bangladesh.

name: chilean-coup
description: General Pinochet overthrew elected socialist President Salvador Allende with US support, beginning 17 years of military dictatorship.

name: soweto-uprising
description: Black South African students protested mandatory Afrikaans instruction. Police killed hundreds, including 12-year-old Hector Pieterson.

name: iran-hostage-crisis
description: Iranian revolutionaries seized the US Embassy in Tehran, holding 52 Americans hostage for 444 days and contributing to Carter's election defeat.

name: john-lennon-killed
description: Former Beatle John Lennon was shot dead outside his New York apartment by Mark David Chapman, who remains imprisoned.

name: assassination-attempt-on-reagan
description: John Hinckley Jr. shot President Reagan outside a Washington hotel, seriously wounding him. Reagan recovered and served two full terms.

name: assassination-attempt-on-pope
description: Turkish gunman Mehmet Ali Agca shot Pope John Paul II in St. Peter's Square. The Pope survived and later forgave his would-be assassin.

name: lebanon-war-1982
description: Israel invaded Lebanon to expel the PLO, beginning an 18-year occupation of southern Lebanon and reshaping Middle Eastern politics.

name: sabra-and-shatila-massacre
description: Lebanese Christian militiamen massacred hundreds to thousands of Palestinians in Beirut refugee camps while Israeli forces surrounded the area.

name: korean-air-lines-007-shot-down
description: Soviet fighters shot down a Korean passenger jet that strayed into Soviet airspace, killing all 269 aboard and escalating Cold War tensions.

name: beirut-barracks-bombing
description: Truck bombs killed 241 US Marines and 58 French paratroopers in Beirut, the deadliest single attack on US Marines since Iwo Jima.

name: assassination-of-indira-gandhi
description: Indian Prime Minister Indira Gandhi was shot by her Sikh bodyguards in retaliation for ordering the assault on the Golden Temple.

name: iran-iraq-war-ends
description: The devastating eight-year war between Iran and Iraq ended in a ceasefire with no territorial changes and over one million dead.

name: panama-invasion
description: US forces invaded Panama to depose dictator Manuel Noriega on drug trafficking charges, the largest American military operation since Vietnam.

name: siege-of-sarajevo-begins
description: Bosnian Serb forces began the longest siege in modern warfare history, lasting nearly four years and killing over 11,000 Sarajevo residents.

name: la-riots
description: Los Angeles erupted in deadly riots after police officers were acquitted of beating Rodney King, causing over $1 billion in damage.

name: us-embassy-bombings
description: Al-Qaeda bombed US embassies in Kenya and Tanzania, killing 224 people and marking a major escalation in jihadist attacks on America.

name: bali-bombings
description: Jemaah Islamiyah terrorists bombed nightclubs in Bali, killing 202 people, mostly Australian tourists, in Indonesia's deadliest terror attack.

name: moscow-theatre-hostage-crisis
description: Chechen militants seized a Moscow theater with 850 hostages. Russian special forces used gas in the rescue, killing 130 hostages.

name: darfur-genocide-begins
description: Sudanese government forces and Janjaweed militias began systematic attacks on non-Arab populations in Darfur, killing an estimated 300,000 people.

name: norway-attacks
description: Far-right terrorist Anders Breivik bombed Oslo and massacred 69 people at a youth camp, Norway's deadliest peacetime attack.

name: boston-marathon-bombing
description: Brothers Tamerlan and Dzhokhar Tsarnaev detonated bombs near the Boston Marathon finish line, killing 3 and injuring over 260.

name: charlie-hebdo-attack
description: Islamist gunmen killed 12 people at the French satirical magazine Charlie Hebdo, sparking global 'Je Suis Charlie' solidarity.

name: san-bernardino-attack
description: A married couple killed 14 people at a San Bernardino holiday party in an ISIS-inspired attack, one of the deadliest US terror attacks since 9/11.

name: nice-truck-attack
description: A terrorist drove a truck through Bastille Day crowds on Nice's Promenade des Anglais, killing 86 people in an ISIS-claimed attack.

name: khashoggi-murder
description: Saudi journalist Jamal Khashoggi was murdered and dismembered inside the Saudi consulate in Istanbul by a team linked to Crown Prince MBS.

name: nagorno-karabakh-war-2020
description: Azerbaijan recaptured most of Nagorno-Karabakh from Armenian forces in a 44-day war featuring extensive drone warfare.

name: sudan-civil-war-2023
description: Fighting erupted between Sudan's army and the Rapid Support Forces paramilitary, causing massive displacement and humanitarian crisis.

name: romanization-britain
description: Emperor Claudius launched the Roman invasion of Britain, establishing a province that lasted nearly four centuries.

name: vercingetorix-gallic-revolt
description: Gallic chieftain Vercingetorix united the tribes against Caesar but was defeated at the Siege of Alesia.

name: julius-caesar-conquest-gaul
description: Caesar's eight-year campaign conquered modern France, expanding Rome's territory and boosting his political power.

name: siege-of-jerusalem-70ce
description: Roman forces under Titus destroyed the Second Temple, ending the Great Jewish Revolt.

name: masada-siege-defense
description: Jewish rebels held out against a Roman siege atop a desert fortress before choosing mass suicide over capture.

name: agricola-britain-conquest
description: Roman governor Agricola pushed deep into Scotland, winning the Battle of Mons Graupius against the Caledonians.

name: xiongnu-conflicts-han
description: Nomadic Xiongnu clashed with the Han Dynasty along China's northern frontier for decades.

name: battle-of-wei-qiao
description: Han Emperor Wu launched an ambush campaign against the Xiongnu, escalating their frontier conflict.

name: han-expansion
description: Emperor Wu expanded Han territory into Central Asia, Korea, and Vietnam through aggressive military campaigns.

name: punic-wars-second
description: Hannibal marched elephants over the Alps to invade Italy, threatening Rome's survival for over a decade.

name: aurelian-reunites-empire
description: Emperor Aurelian defeated the Palmyrene and Gallic empires, reunifying Rome after the Crisis of the Third Century.

name: sima-yan-jin-unification
description: Sima Yan conquered the Wu kingdom, reuniting China under the Jin Dynasty after decades of fragmentation.

name: samudragupta-deccan-campaigns
description: Gupta emperor Samudragupta campaigned across southern India, defeating numerous kings and expanding his empire.

name: aksumite-nubian-conflicts
description: The Aksumite Kingdom of Ethiopia clashed with neighboring Nubian kingdoms over trade routes and territory.

name: great-migration-barbarians
description: Hun pressure drove Germanic tribes westward across Roman borders, destabilizing the Western Roman Empire.

name: stilicho-roman-general
description: Half-Vandal general Stilicho defended the Western Roman Empire against Gothic and other barbarian invasions.

name: stirrup-east-asia
description: The stirrup emerged in China, revolutionizing mounted cavalry warfare across Asia.

name: anglo-saxon-migration
description: Germanic Angles, Saxons, and Jutes migrated to post-Roman Britain, gradually displacing the native Britons.

name: attila-hunnic-invasions
description: Attila the Hun invaded Gaul with a massive army but was halted at the Battle of the Catalaunian Plains.

name: battle-of-catalaunian-plains
description: A Roman-Visigothic coalition stopped Attila's invasion of Gaul in one of antiquity's largest battles.

name: skandagupta-gupta-restoration
description: Gupta emperor Skandagupta repelled Hephthalite Hun invasions, briefly restoring the empire's military strength.

name: hephthalite-invasions
description: The White Huns invaded the Sasanian Empire, killing King Peroz I and destabilizing Persia for decades.

name: battle-of-vouille
description: Frankish king Clovis I defeated the Visigoths, driving them from most of Gaul into Iberia.

name: clovis-frankish-expansion
description: Clovis I defeated the last Roman governor in Gaul at Soissons, founding the Merovingian Frankish state.

name: justinian-gothic-war
description: Byzantine Emperor Justinian launched a campaign to reconquer Italy from the Ostrogoths, devastating the peninsula.

name: belisarius-gothic-war
description: Byzantine general Belisarius captured Rome and much of Italy, though the Ostrogoths soon counterattacked.

name: standing-army
description: Sparta established one of history's first professional standing armies, creating an elite warrior society.

name: gokturk-empire-expansion
description: The Gokturks overthrew the Rouran Khaganate and forged a vast steppe empire from Manchuria to the Black Sea.

name: stirrup-adoption-europe
description: European adoption of the stirrup enabled heavy cavalry charges, transforming medieval warfare and feudalism.

name: early-tang-expansion
description: Emperor Taizong defeated the Eastern Turks and expanded Tang China into Central Asia.

name: heraclius-campaigns-persia
description: Byzantine Emperor Heraclius launched a daring counter-invasion of Persia, winning the decisive Battle of Nineveh.

name: battle-of-mu-ta
description: Early Muslims clashed with Byzantine forces in Transjordan in one of Islam's first major military engagements.

name: siege-of-damascus-636
friendly_name: Siege of Damascus by Arab Forces
description: Arab Muslim forces captured Damascus from the Byzantines, a pivotal early conquest of the Rashidun Caliphate.

name: siege-of-jerusalem-638
friendly_name: Siege of Jerusalem by Arab Forces
description: Caliph Umar accepted Jerusalem's surrender, bringing the holy city under Muslim control for the first time.

name: goguryeo-namgnang-battle
description: Tang China's invasion of Goguryeo was repelled at Ansi Fortress, preserving Korean independence.

name: battle-karbala-husayn
description: Husayn ibn Ali was killed by Umayyad forces, creating the defining martyrdom of Shia Islam.

name: tikal-calakmul-war
description: Tikal defeated Calakmul's king, ending decades of rivalry between the two dominant Maya superpowers.

name: siege-constantinople-arab
description: A massive Arab naval and land assault on Constantinople was repelled, preserving the Byzantine Empire.

name: charles-martel-frankish-victory
description: Frankish leader Charles Martel halted the Umayyad advance into Western Europe near Poitiers.

name: charlemagne-saxon-wars
description: Charlemagne waged a brutal 30-year campaign to conquer and Christianize the pagan Saxons.

name: viking-raids-begin
description: Norse raiders attacked Lindisfarne monastery, marking the start of centuries of Viking expansion.

name: longship-technology
description: Viking longships reached peak design, enabling ocean crossings and shallow river raids across Europe.

name: chain-mail-evolution
description: Chain mail became standard armor for European knights, offering flexible protection in mounted combat.

name: viking-dublin-settlement
description: Norse Vikings established a permanent longphort at Dublin, which became a major trading hub.

name: chola-naval-supremacy
description: Under Rajaraja I, the Chola navy dominated the Indian Ocean, conquering Sri Lanka and the Maldives.

name: gunpowder-china-adoption
description: Chinese armies deployed fire arrows, bombs, and early rockets, pioneering gunpowder warfare.

name: crossbow-adoption-medieval
description: The crossbow became widespread in European warfare, allowing untrained soldiers to pierce heavy armor.

name: castle-fortification-shift
description: Stone castles replaced wooden forts across Europe, transforming defensive warfare and feudal power.

name: rajendra-chola-conquest
description: Rajendra Chola I raided Southeast Asian ports and marched north to the Ganges, extending Chola power.

name: basil-ii-bulgar-victories
friendly_name: Battle of Serres Basil II Victory
description: Emperor Basil II crushed the Bulgarian army at Kleidion and blinded thousands of captives.

name: basil-ii-bulgar-slayer
description: Basil II completed the conquest of Bulgaria, earning the epithet 'Bulgar Slayer' and expanding Byzantium.

name: manzikert-consequences-byzant
description: The Seljuk Turks defeated and captured the Byzantine emperor, opening Anatolia to Turkish settlement.

name: song-gunpowder-weapons
description: The Song Dynasty published the first known gunpowder weapon formulas in the Wujing Zongyao military manual.

name: antioch-crusader-conflicts
description: Crusaders captured Antioch after a grueling siege, establishing one of the major Crusader states.

name: khwarezmian-shah-conquest
description: The Khwarezmian Empire expanded across Central Asia and Persia before provoking the Mongol invasion.

name: second-crusade
description: Kings Louis VII and Conrad III led a failed crusade to recapture Edessa from Muslim forces.

name: dorian-invasion
description: Dorian Greeks migrated into the Peloponnese during the Bronze Age collapse, reshaping Greek civilization.

name: ayyubid-saladin-crusades
description: Saladin founded the Ayyubid dynasty, uniting Egypt and Syria to challenge the Crusader states.

name: montgisard-crusaders-saladin
description: A small Crusader force led by Baldwin IV defeated Saladin's much larger army near Ramla.

name: second-crusade-battle-hattin
description: Saladin annihilated the Crusader army at Hattin, leading to the recapture of Jerusalem.

name: jaffa-crusades
description: Richard the Lionheart defended Jaffa against Saladin during the Third Crusade, securing a coastal truce.

name: rajasthan-kingdoms
description: Rajput kingdoms battled Muslim invaders at the Battles of Tarain, shaping northern India's political future.

name: maya-postclassic-conflicts
description: Postclassic Maya city-states like Mayapan waged wars for dominance after the Classic period collapse.

name: tonga-tongan-conquest
description: The Tu'i Tonga maritime empire expanded across the Pacific, dominating Samoa, Fiji, and nearby islands.

name: iron-stirrups-africa
description: West African kingdoms adopted iron stirrups, enabling cavalry forces that powered Sahel empire expansion.

name: mongolian-stirrup-cavalry
description: Mongol cavalry combined stirrups, composite bows, and tactical discipline into history's most effective mounted force.

name: fourth-crusade-byzantine
description: Crusaders diverted to sack Constantinople, fatally weakening the Byzantine Empire.

name: mongol-conquest-china
description: Genghis Khan invaded the Jin Dynasty, beginning the Mongol conquest that would eventually unify China.

name: damietta-crusade-fifth
description: Crusaders captured the Egyptian port of Damietta but ultimately failed to advance on Cairo.

name: mongol-conquest-khwarezm
description: Genghis Khan destroyed the Khwarezmian Empire after its shah executed Mongol envoys.

name: mongol-invasions-europe
description: Mongol armies swept through Poland and Hungary, devastating European forces at Legnica and Mohi.

name: mongol-invasion-hungary
description: Batu Khan's forces crushed the Hungarian army at Mohi, killing much of the country's population.

name: seventh-crusade
description: King Louis IX of France led a crusade to Egypt but was captured at the Battle of Fariskur.

name: long-bow-english-development
description: English armies adopted the Welsh longbow, creating devastating massed archery tactics.

name: mongol-conquest-bagdad
description: Hulagu Khan sacked Baghdad and killed the Abbasid Caliph, ending the Islamic Golden Age.

name: mongol-naval-expeditions
description: Kublai Khan launched the first Mongol naval invasion of Japan, which was repelled by storms and resistance.

name: mongol-invasion-japan-first
description: Mongol and Korean forces landed at Hakata Bay but withdrew after fierce samurai resistance and a typhoon.

name: mongol-khubilai-conquest
description: Kublai Khan defeated the Southern Song at the naval Battle of Yamen, unifying China under the Yuan Dynasty.

name: mongol-invasion-japan-second
description: A massive Mongol fleet was destroyed by a typhoon, called kamikaze ('divine wind') by the Japanese.

name: acre-final-siege
description: Mamluk forces captured Acre, the last major Crusader stronghold, ending the Crusader presence in the Holy Land.

name: alauddin-khalji-conquest
description: Delhi Sultan Alauddin Khalji conquered much of southern India and repelled multiple Mongol invasions.

name: champa-vietnam-conflicts
description: The Champa kingdom and Dai Viet fought repeatedly, with Mongol invasions further destabilizing the region.

name: maori-pa-fortification
description: Maori tribes built elaborate pa (hilltop forts) with palisades and trenches across New Zealand.

name: catalan-grand-company
description: Catalan mercenaries hired by Byzantium turned rogue, ravaging Greece and seizing the Duchy of Athens.

name: mali-civil-war
description: The Mali Empire fragmented into civil war after Mansa Musa's successors struggled to maintain control.

name: ottoman-standing-army
description: The Ottomans created the Janissary corps, an elite slave-soldier infantry that became Europe's first standing army.

name: timur-tamerlane-conqueror
description: Turco-Mongol conqueror Timur launched devastating campaigns from Persia to India, building a vast empire.

name: gunpowder-weapons-adoption
description: European armies began deploying cannons and early firearms, transforming siege and battlefield tactics.

name: ottoman-military-innovation
description: The Janissaries adopted firearms early, making the Ottoman military one of the first gunpowder armies.

name: oyo-empire-military-innovation
description: The Oyo Empire in West Africa built a powerful cavalry force that dominated the Yoruba region.

name: hussite-wars
description: Czech followers of Jan Hus fought Catholic crusaders using innovative wagon-fort tactics.

name: ottoman-siege-galata
description: Sultan Murad II besieged Constantinople but failed to breach its walls, delaying Ottoman conquest by decades.

name: aztec-expansion-conquests
description: Under Montezuma I, the Aztec Triple Alliance expanded aggressively through central Mexico.

name: inca-expansion
description: Pachacuti transformed the Inca from a small kingdom into the largest empire in the pre-Columbian Americas.

name: gunpowder-military-adoption-europe
description: Cannons and handguns reshaped European warfare, ending the dominance of castles and armored knights.

name: fall-constantinople-1453
description: Ottoman Sultan Mehmed II conquered Constantinople using massive cannons, ending the Byzantine Empire.

name: ottoman-final-siege-1453
description: Mehmed II's 53-day siege breached the Theodosian Walls, ending over a millennium of Roman imperial continuity.

name: hundred-years-war-conclusion
description: France's victory at Castillon ended the 116-year Anglo-French conflict, expelling England from the continent.

name: shimazu-sengoku
description: The Onin War triggered Japan's Sengoku period, a century of civil war among feudal warlords.

name: inca-conquest-expansion
description: Topa Inca Yupanqui conquered the Chimor kingdom and expanded the empire into Ecuador and Chile.

name: inca-tupac-amaru
description: The Inca conquered the Aymara kingdoms around Lake Titicaca, incorporating the Altiplano into their empire.

name: reconquista-completion
description: Ferdinand and Isabella captured Granada, ending nearly 800 years of Muslim rule in Iberia.

name: italian-wars
description: France invaded Italy, triggering decades of conflict that reshaped European diplomacy and warfare.

name: songhai-expansion
description: Askia Muhammad expanded the Songhai Empire across West Africa, creating the region's largest state.

name: oromo-expansion
description: Oromo pastoralists migrated northward through the Ethiopian highlands, reshaping the region's demographics.

name: ming-great-wall
description: The Ming Dynasty rebuilt and extended the Great Wall with brick and stone to defend against Mongol raids.

name: songhai-military-gunpowder
description: The Songhai Empire employed gunpowder weapons acquired through trans-Saharan trade networks.

name: inca-civil-war-dispute
description: Brothers Huascar and Atahualpa fought a devastating civil war for the Inca throne, weakening the empire.

name: kanem-bornu-gunpowder-adoption
description: The Kanem-Bornu Empire acquired Ottoman firearms, strengthening its military in the Lake Chad region.

name: songhai-military-organization
description: Askia Muhammad organized the Songhai military into specialized provincial units with professional officers.

name: aztec-expansion-reign
description: Montezuma II expanded the Aztec Empire to its greatest extent through campaigns in Oaxaca and beyond.

name: mombasa-portuguese-control
description: Portuguese forces sacked and occupied Mombasa, asserting control over Indian Ocean trade routes.

name: shaybanid-bukhara-captured
description: Uzbek Shaybanid forces captured Bukhara and Samarkand, ending Timurid rule in Central Asia.

name: portuguese-goa-conquest
description: Afonso de Albuquerque captured Goa, establishing Portugal's most important base in Asia.

name: portuguese-calicut-control
description: Portugal bombarded and dominated Calicut's trade, enforcing a monopoly on Indian Ocean spice routes.

name: safavid-battled-ottomans
description: The Ottoman victory at Chaldiran established the border between Sunni Ottoman and Shia Safavid empires.

name: spanish-conquest-aztec
description: Hernan Cortes and indigenous allies besieged and captured Tenochtitlan, destroying the Aztec Empire.

name: madura-sultanate-fall
description: The Madura Sultanate on Java was overthrown by the expanding Majapahit Empire.

name: spanish-tercio-formation
description: Spain formalized the tercio, combining pikemen and musketeers into Europe's dominant infantry formation.

name: inca-conflict-spanish
description: Pizarro captured Atahualpa at Cajamarca during the Inca civil war, beginning Spain's conquest of Peru.

name: spanish-conquest-peru
description: Pizarro executed Atahualpa and captured Cusco, toppling the Inca Empire.

name: araucanians
description: Mapuche warriors defeated Spanish conquistadors at Curalaba, beginning centuries of resistance in Chile.

name: mapuche-arauco-war
description: The Mapuche fought a prolonged guerrilla war against Spanish colonizers in southern Chile.

name: mughal-akbar
description: Emperor Akbar won the Second Battle of Panipat and expanded Mughal rule across most of the Indian subcontinent.

name: wars-of-religion-france
description: Decades of civil war between French Catholics and Huguenots devastated France and killed millions.

name: oda-nobunaga-unification
description: Warlord Oda Nobunaga began unifying Japan through military innovation, including mass use of firearms.

name: ottoman-janissary-corps
description: The Janissary corps underwent reorganization as firearms became central to Ottoman military doctrine.

name: toyotomi-hideo-invasions
description: Hideyoshi launched a massive invasion of Korea with 160,000 troops, aiming to conquer Ming China.

name: korean-imjin-war
description: Japan invaded Korea, but Admiral Yi Sun-sin's naval victories and Ming reinforcements repelled the attack.

name: imjin-war-turtle-ship
description: Admiral Yi Sun-sin deployed armored turtle ships to devastating effect against the Japanese navy.

name: mombasa-fort
description: The Portuguese built Fort Jesus to secure Mombasa, a key stronghold on the East African coast.

name: imjin-war-second-invasion
description: Hideyoshi's second Korean invasion stalled after his death, ending Japan's continental ambitions.

name: russo-swedish-wars
description: Russia and Sweden fought over Baltic territory, with Sweden seizing Ingria and parts of Karelia.

name: spanish-armada-aftermath
description: Spain's naval power declined after the Armada defeat and subsequent failed campaigns against England.

name: russian-expansion-siberia
description: Cossack leader Yermak crossed the Urals to defeat the Siberian Khanate, beginning Russia's eastward expansion.

name: trinh-nguyen-civil-war
description: Vietnam split between the Trinh lords in the north and Nguyen lords in the south for over a century.

name: shimabara-rebellion
description: Japanese Christian peasants revolted against persecution; their defeat led to Japan's isolation policy.

name: portuguese-restoration-war
description: Portugal revolted against Spanish rule, beginning a 28-year war to restore independence.

name: swedish-empire-peak
description: Sweden under Gustavus Adolphus dominated Northern Europe through innovative tactics in the Thirty Years' War.

name: qing-expansion
description: Manchu forces captured Beijing and established the Qing Dynasty, conquering all of China over decades.

name: louis-xiv-expansion
description: Louis XIV invaded the Spanish Netherlands in the War of Devolution, beginning decades of French expansion.

name: asante-confederacy-military
description: The Asante Confederacy built a powerful military with firearms, dominating the Gold Coast region.

name: llanquihue-battle
description: Mapuche forces defeated Spanish settlers in southern Chile during a widespread indigenous uprising.

name: aurangzeb-expansion-begin
description: Mughal emperor Aurangzeb launched a prolonged campaign to conquer the Deccan sultanates and crush the Marathas.

name: danish-swedish-wars
description: Denmark and Sweden fought repeatedly for Baltic dominance, with Sweden marching across the frozen Great Belt.

name: maratha-resistance-begins
description: Shivaji was crowned Chhatrapati, founding the Maratha Empire and challenging Mughal dominance in India.

name: peter-great-reforms
description: Peter the Great rebuilt Russia's army along European lines after his Grand Embassy tour of the West.

name: kangxi-military-campaigns
description: Emperor Kangxi suppressed the Three Feudatories rebellion and later conquered Taiwan and fought the Dzungars.

name: deccan-campaigns-mughal
description: Aurangzeb captured and executed Maratha king Sambhaji, but guerrilla resistance drained Mughal resources.

name: qing-conquers-mongolia
description: Emperor Kangxi personally led campaigns that defeated the Dzungar Mongols at Zuunmod.

name: sikh-khalsa-panth
description: Guru Gobind Singh founded the Khalsa, transforming the Sikh community into a martial brotherhood.

name: prussian-discipline-training
description: Frederick William I created Prussia's rigorous military drill system, building Europe's most disciplined army.

name: military-academy-russia
description: Russia established the Noble Cadet Corps in St. Petersburg, formalizing military officer training.

name: sikh-mughal-conflicts
description: Sikh warrior bands clashed with Mughal forces across Punjab after the execution of Guru Tegh Bahadur.

name: asante-akwamu-conquest
description: The expanding Asante Confederacy defeated the Akwamu kingdom, consolidating power on the Gold Coast.

name: maroon-wars-jamaica
friendly_name: Maroon Wars for Freedom in Jamaica
description: Jamaican Maroons fought the British to a peace treaty recognizing their autonomy and land rights.

name: frederick-great-militarism
description: Frederick the Great of Prussia introduced oblique order tactics and invaded Silesia, sparking European wars.

name: qing-xinjiang-conquest
description: The Qing Dynasty conquered the Dzungar Khanate and incorporated Xinjiang, nearly doubling China's territory.

name: burmese-siamese-wars
description: Burma and Siam fought devastating wars, culminating in the Burmese destruction of Ayutthaya.

name: ayutthaya-fall
description: Burmese forces sacked Ayutthaya, destroying the 400-year-old Siamese capital and its cultural treasures.

name: tay-son-rebellion
description: Three brothers led a peasant uprising that overthrew both the Nguyen and Trinh lords, briefly unifying Vietnam.

name: washington-appointed-commander
description: The Continental Congress appointed George Washington as commander-in-chief of the Continental Army.

name: valley-forge-winter
description: Washington's army endured a brutal winter at Valley Forge but emerged as a disciplined fighting force.

name: yorktown-surrender
description: British General Cornwallis surrendered at Yorktown, effectively ending the American Revolutionary War.

name: war-declared-austria
description: Revolutionary France declared war on Austria, beginning over two decades of European revolutionary warfare.

name: barbary-wars
description: The United States fought North African pirates to end tribute payments and protect American shipping.

name: napoleonic-wars-begin
description: Napoleon's France fought a series of coalition wars that reshaped the political map of Europe.

name: peninsular-war
description: Napoleon invaded Spain, provoking a brutal guerrilla war that drained French resources for six years.

name: napoleon-retreat-russia
description: Napoleon invaded Russia with 600,000 troops but retreated in devastating winter conditions, losing most of his army.

name: battle-waterloo-napoleon
description: Wellington and Blucher defeated Napoleon at Waterloo, ending his rule and two decades of European war.

name: zulu-military-innovation
description: Shaka Zulu revolutionized warfare with the short stabbing spear and disciplined regiment formations.

name: mfecane-wars
description: The Mfecane devastated southern Africa as Zulu expansion triggered mass migrations and widespread conflict.

name: zulu-impis-regiment
description: Shaka organized Zulu warriors into age-based impis using the 'horns of the buffalo' encirclement tactic.

name: sotho-moshoeshoe-reforms
description: King Moshoeshoe I united the Sotho people at Thaba Bosiu, using diplomacy and defense to survive the Mfecane.

name: maori-musket-wars
description: Maori tribes with European muskets launched devastating raids, reshaping New Zealand's tribal landscape.

name: opium-wars-second
description: Britain and France forced China to open more ports and legalize the opium trade after military defeat.

name: american-civil-war
description: Confederate forces fired on Fort Sumter, beginning a four-year war over slavery and national unity.

name: blitzkrieg-tactics-deployed
description: Germany's invasion of Poland showcased blitzkrieg warfare, combining tanks, planes, and infantry for rapid victory.

name: atomic-bomb-nagasaki-ww2
description: The US dropped a plutonium bomb on Nagasaki three days after Hiroshima, hastening Japan's surrender.

name: korean-war-begins-1950
description: North Korea invaded the South, drawing in UN and Chinese forces in a Cold War proxy conflict.

name: hungarian-uprising-1956
description: Hungarians revolted against Soviet control, but Soviet tanks crushed the uprising within weeks.

name: vietnam-war-escalates-1964
description: The Gulf of Tonkin incident led to massive US military escalation in Vietnam.
